mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, it indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate profit growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0% over a 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand expansion, which drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of aligned observation windows over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects resilient manufacturing output that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months where INDPRO rises during identified late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215.0, it indicates accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate margins and forces markets to price in tighter future monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0, invalidating the inflation penalty effect.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly increases in real retail sales confirm a synchronized demand recovery, generating positive short-term equity momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 58% of aligned observation windows during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that precedes broader economic slowdown and corporate earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO.
